---
title: "ICE-BUFFER — Glacier Buffering of Streamflow Droughts"
status: active
start: 2025
---


# ICE-BUFFER: Global Glacier Buffering of Streamflow Droughts Using Deep Learning

**Status:** Active (2025–2028)  
**Funding:** FWO Postdoctoral Junior Fellowship  
**Institution:** Vrije Universiteit Brussel  
**Supervisor:** Harry Zekollari

## Summary

Glaciers act as natural water reservoirs, releasing meltwater during warm and dry periods and thus buffering streamflow droughts. The ICE-BUFFER project uses deep learning approaches to quantify this glacier buffering effect globally and project how it will change under future climate scenarios.

## Methods

- Deep learning models (neural networks, LSTM) for hydrological prediction in glacierized basins
- Global-scale analysis of glacier runoff regimes and drought buffering capacity
- CMIP6-driven future projections
- Hybrid physics-informed ML approaches

## Datasets

- Glacier inventory and mass balance data (RGI, WGMS)
- Global streamflow observations (GAGES-II, GRDC)
- ERA5 reanalysis
- CMIP6 climate projections

## Related Publications

- Ponds et al. (2026, in review) — Irrigation buffers glacier loss

## Code

- [GitHub](https://github.com/rodaguayo)
